author: Jagat Singh, Master
title: Kheti ki Tisri Kitab
notes: '(Designed for educational purposes.)' printed above entry. Completes the three-part agriculture catechism (p23 s160–161); consecutive regs 1270–1272.

author: Jagat Singh, Master
title: Khulasa-i-Geographical Reader
gloss: Or an abstract of the Geographical Reader for the second lower primary class.
notes: Author dash-continuation of s162. '(Designed for educational purposes.)' printed above entry. Master Jagat Singh's cram-abstract line now spans agriculture and geography.

author: Joshi, M. S., Jagranwi
title: Vishvodharak Directory
gloss: Or the directory of 130 Arya Samajes, giving the names of the towns where they are and containing the names and addresses of the editors of some newspapers and of other gentlemen.
notes: A directory of 130 Arya Samaj branches plus newspaper editors, printed at the Naqshbandi Mujaddidi Press — the movement's organizational network made legible (and our best single snapshot of Arya institutional density in 1910).

author: Kapur, K. S., Mr.
title: Do sau salah Jantri san 1901 laghayat 2100
copyright: M. Kishan Singh, Amritsar; No. 198, dated the 12th December, 1910.
notes: A single-sheet 200-year calendar (1901–2100), copyrighted — broadside ephemera claiming a two-century horizon. Reg 1410 = highest registration number recorded in 1910 so far.

flags.issue: proprietor name partially cut at page edge; read as M. Kishan Singh

author: Karamatullah, Mir
title: Daur-i-Saghir
notes: Banker-financed temperance again (cf. p22 s28, banker at Peshawar) — NWF-frontier commercial patrons funding free moral print through Amritsar presses.

author: Lachman Singh, Sodhi
title: Risala-i-Jauhar-i-Kaghzat-i-Zamin
gloss: A booklet on land records for Primary Schools.
notes: '(Designed for educational purposes.)' printed above entry. Revenue-record literacy (patwari paperwork) taught in primary school — the colonial land bureaucracy reproducing itself through the curriculum.

title: Maqasid wa Qwaid Sarb Sadharan Sahaita Bhandar, Ferozepore
gloss: Rules and objects of the Sarb Sadharan Sahaita or Mutual Relief Fund, Ferozepore.
publisher: Dr. Pars Ram, Managing Director of the Fund
notes: 'Sarb Sadharan' (all-common) mutual relief fund constitution — vernacular associational capitalism; compare the 3-script fund circular in Q3.

author: Muhammad Abdul Ghani, Sayad
title: Ghar ka Rafiq
notes: Author registered from Bijnor, United Provinces — another out-of-province registration through Lahore.
